s4r3-fem-irreg-w2.ans: Because -if adjectives follow Pattern B: the f "voices" to v, then -e is added — like English knife → knives.

s1-gender-w5.ans: Because the -tion ending is a reliable feminine gender marker in French

s4r4-plural-adj-w2.ans: Because French defaults to masculine for mixed-gender groups — if any noun in the group is masculine, the adjective takes masculine plural.

attempts.you: Because French defaults to masculine for mixed-gender groups — if any noun in the group is masculine, the adjective takes masculine plural.

s4r4-plural-adj-w1.ans: Because adjectives ending in -x (or -s) already have a plural-sounding ending, so nothing is added.

s2b-indefinite-w1.ans: The grammatical gender of the noun — masculine nouns take "un", feminine nouns take "une".

attempts.you: The grammatical gender of the noun — masculine nouns take "un", feminine nouns take "une".

s2a-elision-w1.ans: Because "homme" starts with a vowel sound — the h is silent (mute), and French uses elision to avoid two vowel sounds colliding.

attempts.you: Because "homme" starts with a vowel sound — the h is silent (mute), and French uses elision to avoid two vowel sounds colliding.

s3-plurals-w2.ans: Because nouns already ending in -s, -x, or -z don't add anything — they already "look" plural.

attempts.you: Because nouns already ending in -s, -x, or -z don't add anything — they already "look" plural.

s1-gender-w2.ans: "Table" is feminine, so it needs "la" — the article must match the noun's gender, which is masculine (le) or feminine (la).

s4r3-fem-irreg-w1.ans: Because -eux adjectives follow their own pattern: the x drops, s replaces it, and -e is added — giving -euse.

s4r1-position-w1.ans: Because in French, most adjectives are placed AFTER the noun — the opposite of English word order.

attempts.you: Because in French, most adjectives are placed AFTER the noun — the opposite of English word order.

s3-plurals-w4.ans: There are exactly 7 special -ou words that take -x; all others follow the regular -s rule

s3-plurals-w1.ans: Because nouns ending in -al have their own plural pattern: -al changes to -aux.

s4r3-fem-irreg-w3.ans: Because doubling the n preserves the vowel sound — without the double n, "bone" would change the pronunciation of the "o".

attempts.you: Because doubling the n preserves the vowel sound — without the double n, "bone" would change the pronunciation of the "o".

s4r2-fem-basic-w1.ans: Because French adjectives must agree in gender with the noun they describe — adding -e marks the feminine form.

s3-plurals-w3.ans: Because the definite article must agree in number with the noun — singular le/la becomes plural les.
